_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of cyber attackers (e.g., script kiddies, insider threat, non-nation state sponsored, and nation sponsored).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI-specific attack taxonomy, including adversarial examples, model evasion, model extraction, model inversion, membership inference, data poisoning, and prompt injection.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-K-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-S-002 (RM, cosine: 0.485), SP-K-012 (SP, cosine: 0.482).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.417). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [attackers, cyber, insider, kiddies, nation, non] vs WIDAI-only terms [adversarial, ai, attack, data, evasion, examples] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.858 (P=0.860, R=0.856). Balanced P/R — the 15-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.7%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of cyber attackers (e.g., script kiddies, insider threat, non-nation s" and "Knowledge of AI-specific attack taxonomy, including adversarial examples, model". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.536 > STS 0.417 (gap +0.119). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.4%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [attackers, cyber, insider, kiddies, nation, non]. WIDAI-only: [adversarial, ai, attack, data, evasion, examples].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.7% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
